title: Product Context (merged PRD.md + .factory/stack.yaml)
description: Contract for the machine context a product exposes to Factory skills. A product is authored in TWO files split by author: PRD.md frontmatter (human-owned: product, domain, meta) and .factory/stack.yaml (machine-owned, written by /plan-arch: tech_stack, commands, skills, guardrails, escalation_policy, tech_bindings). `fac sync-context` merges them, derives the compatibility aliases, validates the result against this schema, and writes .factory/context.gen.yaml. Each property carries an `x-owner` annotation naming the file that authors it. This schema is a genuine superset of the agent-skills project-context schema: every key that library defines is reproduced below so vendored craft skills bind unchanged.

product.description: Product identity. Authored by a human in PRD.md frontmatter.

name.description: Product name. Non-empty — an untouched template must not validate.

project.description: COMPAT ALIAS — do not author. Derived from `product` by `fac sync-context` because the agent-skills library requires a `project` key. Vendored craft skills bind to this.

domain.description: One-line domain statement. Sets the frame for domain-knowledge craft skills.

tech_stack.description: Languages allowed for this product and the per-component choices made at design time by /plan-arch. Drives Implementer craft-skill routing.

languages.description: Allowed language set for this product.

commands.description: Per-component command config (read -> ask -> persist). Keys are component names plus optional top-level 'deploy'.

tech_bindings.description: Concrete infrastructure a skill may target. `fac sync-context` derives entries from tech_stack.components[] (per-component stack, plus `db`); values authored here win over derived ones. Required by library skills that bind ${ctx.tech_bindings}.

cache.description: Caching layer, e.g. 'redis', 'none'. Audited for cache-poisoning / authz-bypass (OWASP A01/A05).

auth.description: Authn/authz provider or strategy, e.g. 'auth0', 'oidc', 'session-cookie', 'jwt'. Audited by /security (access management, RBAC, tokens — OWASP A01/A07, API BOLA/BFLA).

crypto.description: Encryption / key-management binding, e.g. 'aws-kms', 'libsodium', 'webcrypto'. The Factory rolls no crypto itself; audited for weak/rolled-your-own crypto (OWASP A02).

session.description: Session strategy, e.g. 'stateless-jwt', 'redis-session', 'signed-cookie'. Audited for fixation / weak lifetime (OWASP A07).

tls.description: Transport security posture for public endpoints, e.g. 'lets-encrypt', 'acm', 'cloudflare'. /deploy hard-gates a public deploy on a valid chain + min TLS version + HSTS via lib/tls-verify.ts (OWASP A02/A05).

skills.description: Manifest of skills this product has adopted, with optional per-skill overrides.

name.description: Skill folder name (Layer 1 workflow or vendored craft).

version.description: Pinned version copied from source (craft skills).

layer.description: Architecture layer this skill maps to.

budget.description: Per-run cost budget (plan §8.1). Warns, does not halt, until a measured baseline exists.

escalation_policy.description: When and how to escalate / defer to humans.

jurisdictions.description: LIBRARY KEY — applicable jurisdictions/regions, most-relevant first.

authority_hierarchy.description: LIBRARY KEY — source-authority tiers, ORDERED highest to lowest.

sources.description: LIBRARY KEY — source registry grouped by tier.

glossary.description: LIBRARY KEY — domain terminology the agent should use/expand.

compliance_rules.description: LIBRARY KEY — non-negotiable rules skills must honour.

confidence_tiers.description: LIBRARY KEY — authority level to user-facing confidence label.
